sections.paragraph:
a root of doubtful authority, as a verb; as far as may be gathered from the derivatives-

(1) to go in a circle (kindred to טוּר, תּוּר, דּוּר, שׁוּר), whence צִיר hinge, writhing pain.

(2) to go (Arab. صار Med. Ye, to go, to arrive, comp. סוּר, זוּר), whence צִיר a messenger. Hence-

Hithpael הִצְטַיִּרִ Jos 9:4, may be, “they betook themselves to the way.” But no other trace of this form and signification is either found in Hebrew, or in Aramean , and the ancient interpreters have all given it as הִצְטַיָּד֑וּ “they furnished themselves with provisions for the journey,” as in ver. 12 Jos 9:12, which appears to me preferable.

I. צִיר m.-

(1) the hinge of a door, Pro 26:14, from the root צִיר No. 1. From the same-

(2) צִירִים i.q. חֲבָלִים pains (as if writhings) of a parturient woman, Isa 13:8 21:3 1Sa 4:19. Metaph. used of terror, Dan 10:16, which is often compared with the pain and alarm in childbirth; compare ضار V. to twist oneself with pain.

(3) a messenger, Pro 13:17 25:13 .

II. צִיר m. (from the root צוּר No. 5)-

(1) an idol, Isa 45:16.

(2) form, Psa 49:15 כתיב
